description: A traditional Japanese soup made with miso dissolved in dashi, often served with tofu, seaweed, and scallions. It is light in texture but savory and deeply seasoned.
preparation_steps: Heat the dashi without boiling aggressively.
Add tofu and other quick-cooking ingredients.
Dissolve miso paste in a small amount of warm broth.
Stir the miso back into the pot near the end of cooking.
Serve hot, often with scallions on top.

health_advice.benefits: Can be light, warming, and a simple way to include tofu and fermented ingredients.
health_advice.risks: Sodium can be high depending on the amount of miso and type of dashi used.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Keep portions moderate if you are watching salt intake and avoid oversalting the broth.

dietary_notes.note: Traditional dashi often contains fish, so vegetarian or vegan versions require kombu- or mushroom-based stock. Some miso varieties may contain barley or other gluten-containing grains.

allergens: Possible gluten in some miso varieties
